title: Battle of Rodia (Yuuzhan Vong War)

{{Quote|Once again, a Mid Rim world is sacrificed in the name of protecting the Core ... or Bothawui.|[[Navik/Legends|Navik]] to [[Borsk Fey'lya]]|<ref name="BP" />}}
During their own [[Yuuzhan Vong War|conquest crusade]] against all worlds of the [[Galaxy/Legends|Galaxy]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] race of invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], '''attacked''' [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], [[Druckenwell/Legends|Druckenwell]], [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]], and [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]] in an attempt to cut off the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]]. To further their conquest of Rodia, the extragalactic aliens [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] the planet and then turned their attention to its native inhabitants. The [[Shaper caste|Yuuzhan Vong shapers]] used the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians']] genetic stock to create monstrosities such as the crab-legged [[Vagh Rodiek]], a new race of [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] soldiers.

==History==
===Prelude===
{{Quote|After all, Commander, business is business, and if anyone is to profit from the galaxy’s new circumstances, it may as well be the Hutts|[[Borga Besadii Diori]], to [[Malik Carr]]|<ref name="JE">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref>}}
In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] race of invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], launched a massive [[Yuuzhan Vong War|conquest campaign]] directed against all worlds of the [[Galactic community]].<ref name="VP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref> [[26 ABY/Legends|Less than a year later]],<ref name="IYV" /> the Yuuzhan Vong had provided numerous examples showing that those populations who mounted resistance saw their worlds poisoned or devastated. Many inhabitants throughout the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], and further to the edge of the galaxy were rapidly losing faith in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic’s]] ability to bring the war to an end. At that time, the Yuuzhan Vong were preparing a major offensive – their first since the [[battle of Ithor]] – and required more worlds for resource gathering.<ref name="JE" /> The [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species, who possessed [[Hutt Space/Legends|vast territories]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="EA" /> in an attempt to avoid bloodshed entirely, considered striking deals with the Yuuzhan Vong and were willing to cede some of their own planets to do so.<ref name="JE" />

[[File:BorgaAndHutts.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wishing to gain profits from their cooperation with the Yuuzhan Vong, the Hutts concluded a pact with the invaders]]
During the negotiations on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], [[Borga Besadii Diori]], who had been vested with the authority by the leaders of the voting [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidics]] to represent the interests of all Hutts before the Yuuzhan Vong, agreed to cooperate with the invaders. In exchange for the granting of certain worlds, the Hutts were to request that the Yuuzhan Vong keep clear of Rimward Hutt Space in general, as well as avoid the worlds of [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]], [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], and several planets in the [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]] and the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] so as not to interfere with their trade in [[Spice/Legends|spice]] and other proscribed goods.<ref name="JE" />

Both sides of the negotiations assured each other of the reliability of the agreement, but the hidden motives on both sides were evident, and the fragility of the pact was apparent from the start. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|Intelligence agents]] of the New Republic were aware of the fact that the negotiations on Nal Hutta had taken place, but not of their content. Even the [[Grand Protector/Legends|ruler]] of Rodia, [[Navik/Legends|Navik the Red]], apparently did not know that the Hutts had secured immunity for his homeworld, and in the Advisory Council chambers on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] he discussed with the [[Chief of State (New Republic)|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]] and other council members the necessity of defending [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], due to its proximity to Rodia.<ref name="JE" />

===Invasion===
{{Quote|My homeworld is about to suffer the most terrible depredations—|The rules of Rodia, Navik|<ref name="BP" />}}
Under the terms of the agreement, the Yuuzhan Vong shared information with the Hutts about which planets they planned to attack next, on the condition that this knowledge remained confidential. Nevertheless, Borga and her fellow Hutts sought to act as conduits of information for the New Republic. After the extragalactic invaders were [[Battle of Fondor|defeated]] at [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], the Hutts' double-dealing was exposed, and the Yuuzhan Vong Commander [[Nas Choka]] retaliated by swiftly conquering all of Hutt Space.<ref name="CE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> While Borga Besadii Diori stayed behind to [[Battle of Nal Hutta|defend]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], many members of her kajidic had already evacuated to [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]], Tatooine, and Rodia,<ref name="BP" /> even though these worlds could no longer avoid attack under the terms of the now-broken alliance.<ref name="JE" />

[[File:RodiaSurroundingsAtCorellianRun.jpg|right|thumb|250px|The Rimward planets along the Corellian Run came under threat of attack after the fall of Kalarba]]
In an attempt to cut off the Rimward leg of the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]], a vital [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]],<ref name="BP" /> the Yuuzhan Vong launched a blitz across the [[Doldur sector/Legends|Doldur sector]]<ref name="EA" /> and [[Battle of Kalarba|devastated]] [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]]. The isolated worlds along the Corellian Run toward the Outer Rim Territories went on full alert as the Yuuzhan Vong captured them one after another. Confidence in the strength of these worlds had been low, and other star systems not yet touched by the war expected an influx of [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] refugees.<ref name="BP" />

Eventually, after the fall of [[Battle of Druckenwell (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Druckenwell]] and [[Battle of Falleen (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Falleen]], and as a new wave of [[yorik coral]] warships struck the Corellian Run, it became clear that the invaders were advancing toward Rodia. Despite the growing threat to Rodia, Chief of State Borsk Fey’lya managed to keep the remnants of the [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|Fifth Fleet]] stationed at Bothawui, to the powerless frustration of Navik the Red, the Councilor from Rodia. In turn, the [[Director of Fleet Intelligence|Director]] of [[New Republic Fleet Intelligence]], [[Ayddar Nylykerka]], expressed doubts about the possibility of holding Rodia.<ref name="BP" />

Soon afterward, the New Republic withdrew from Rodia,<ref name="BP" /> allowing the Yuuzhan Vong to occupy the [[Tyrius system/Legends|star system]] with ease.<ref name="NJOS" /> [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senator]] [[Viqi Shesh]], who sought to become the head of a new collaborationist government,<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> contacted the [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] in an attempt to bring the war to a peaceful end. She described the simultaneous withdrawal from Rodia and [[Battle of Kubindi|Kubindi]] as a gesture of goodwill, but her proposal was ignored. Some time later, Tsavong Lah briefly visited the surrendered world of Rodia aboard his flagship, the ''[[Sunulok]]'', before departing the planet for Duro,<ref name="BP" /> which the Yuuzhan Vong regarded as a primary staging ground for their push into the Core.<ref name="CE" />

===Aftermath===
====Social influence====
Many of the worlds and star systems conquered at that time, including Rodia, had been home to some of the most visible and active starfaring species. Whether by design on the part of the invaders or merely by coincidence, this strategy gave further fuel to the arguments of [[Speciesism|non-Human activists]]. The most paranoid believed that the New Republic leadership had somehow orchestrated a conspiracy to do what the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] had failed to do – to kill all non-Humans.<ref name="NJOS" />

====Rodia project====
[[File:Equator_City.png|left|thumb|Equator City on Rodia was destroyed during the occupation]]
When Rodia fell, the [[Shaper caste|Yuuzhan Vong shapers]] immediately descended upon the verdant sphere.<ref name="IYV" /> They [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] Rodia itself, crumbling the urban blocks of [[Equator City/Legends|Equator City]] to seed the forests with leathery, [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]-sized blossoms,<ref name="EA" /> and obliterated other polluted Rodian [[City/Legends|cities]] with [[Metal/Legends|metal]]-eating microbes, then planting [[damutek]] laboratories in the open [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]].<ref name="IYV" /> Many Rodians did not cooperate with the invaders and withdrew into the trees. These freedom fighters continued to put up fierce resistance on their heavily jungled homeworld. Although the widespread industrial complexes around Rodian urban centers disgusted the Yuuzhan Vong, [[Warrior caste|their warriors]] developed a grudging respect for the violence-loving Rodians. This opinion became so common that perhaps the Rodians might have been able to assume a role within Yuuzhan Vong society as favored [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] – once they had been subdued and properly shaped.<ref name="NJOS" />

[[File:Vagh_Rodiek_G8.png|right|thumb|250px|The awful image of the Yuuzhan Vong occupation of Rodia manifested in dreadful beasts known as Vagh Rodiek]]
The [[Master Shaper]] [[Taug Molou]] received orders from [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] to create a tractable slave species on par with the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Chazrach]], as part of the «Rodia project» – an initiative aimed at developing a new class of obedient soldiers for the Yuuzhan Vong. Master Molou was not willing simply to control the Rodians with [[Yorik-Kul|coral implants]]. He wished to shape a completely new species and was not above straining [[Shaping protocol|religious protocols]] to achieve it.<ref name="IYV" /> In a planetwide project, the Yuuzhan Vong shapers merged Rodian biological material with that of other creatures to create<ref name="NEGAS" /> monstrosities such as the crab-legged [[Vagh Rodiek]].<ref name="NEC" /> Thousands of Rodians died in vivisection chambers before the first Vagh Rodiek emerged from its [[birthing blister]]. These former Rodians were nothing more than animals—mindless drones without guidance from their Yuuzhan Vong masters. The Vagh Rodiek were made available to military commanders, particularly those operating near [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], when Chazrach were unavailable. However, the Vagh Rodiek did not exist until some time after the fall of Rodia and did not become common until the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall of Coruscant]].<ref name="IYV" />

====Further destiny of the planet====
As the Rodians resisted the occupation on their homeworld, their representative in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senate]] spoke out against the New Republic’s failure to come to their aid. Rodians across the galaxy armed themselves and prepared to return home. The Yuuzhan Vong were expected to face a far larger force than they had encountered before.<ref name="NJOS" /> Nevertheless, the Rodian resistance did not attain success, and Rodia was not liberated before the end of the war,<ref name="EA" /> when the [[Sekot accords|surrender]] of the invaders was signed.<ref name="UP">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> Afterward, Rodia joined the successor state to the New Republic, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]].<ref name="DN">''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]''</ref>

Despite the crimes of the invaders, Rodia continued to attract Yuuzhan Vong [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] long after the war and became home to one of the largest such populations.<ref name="EA" /> After a little more than a [[130 ABY|hundred years]], Rodia was regarded as one of the most dangerous planets in the galaxy. Its wildlife had been drastically manipulated by the Yuuzhan Vong, and the planet’s rainforests teemed with deadly [[Diet/Legends|predators]] previously unknown to the galaxy. Fortunately, this suited the Rodian people just fine, as it had given them new creatures to stalk and hunt. Over the following century, the people of Rodia reveled in the dangers introduced to their world and became even greater hunters than ever before.<ref name="LE">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The capture of Rodia was first mentioned in the sixth novel of the [[New Jedi Order]] series, ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'', which was published in [[2000]]. The event was briefly described, providing only a tense backdrop for the narrative rather than detailed coverage. Later sources depicting Rodia and its inhabitants during the Yuuzhan Vong War referred only to events that occurred during or after the occupation by the invaders, while the specific details of the battle itself remained undisclosed.
